channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Stacey Abrams slammed after defending $2 billion in Biden-era EPA funds to buy green energy appliances
item.description: Former Democratic Georgia gubernatorial candidate Stacey Abrams is under fire from conservatives after defending a $2 billion initiative under the Biden EPA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: D.C.'s Black Lives Matter mural will be erased. Look back at the iconic street painting
item.description: Over the past five years, Washington, D.C.'s iconic Black Lives Matter street painting has served as a powerful symbol of activism and a gathering place for joy and resistance.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its employees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most of the 80,000 federal workers responsible for researching diseases, inspecting food and administering Medicare and Medicaid under the auspices of the Health and Human Services Department were emailed an offer to leave their job for as much as a $25,000 payment as part of Presi
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk and DOGE try to slash government by cutting out those who answer to voters
item.description: Conservatives have long talked about cutting government, but Republicans in Congress have shied away from approving deep reductions because of fear of how voters would react.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's transactional approach to diplomacy is a driving force on the world stage
item.description: Donald Trump's style of diplomacy is a whole lot like a high-stakes business deal, and his No. 1 goal is to come out of the transaction on top.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump shifts tone on Musk as tensions rise with Cabinet
item.description: President Trump is signaling a softer approach to the mass firings of federal government workers led by Elon Musk as cracks emerge between the tech billionaire and Cabinet secretaries running the agencies he’s gutting.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump order denies student loan relief to nonprofit workers engaged in 'improper' activity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is ordering changes to the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program that would disqualify workers of nonprofit groups deemed to have engaged in “improper” activities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican lawmakers hold telephone town halls as protesters demand more
item.description: HOLLAND, Mich. (AP) — Protesters demanding an in-person town hall from their western Michigan GOP congressman chanted loudly Friday as honking drivers signaled support, disrupting the usual calm of a conservative family vacation town. Mere hours later, Rep. Bill Huizenga held a town hall — by phone.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Singing Into Irrelevancy
item.description: Gump logic: Forrest Gump’s mama was right – stupid is as stupid does. ICYMI: For those that don't know, ICYMI is how the cool kids say “In case you missed it.” Sibling shoutout: The smarter and more talented Vittert child – aka my sister, Liberty – is out with a must-read in The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Protesters denounce ICE arrests at St. Louis Park manufacturing facility
item.description: Dozens of people gathered outside a St. Louis Park aluminum finishing facility Wednesday night to protest recent federal immigration arrests at the company.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox and Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: Hispanic Caucus demands apology from GOP campaign arm after ‘illegal immigrant’ smear
item.description: The Congressional Hispanic Caucus (CHC) is demanding an apology from the House Republicans’ campaign arm after it falsely portrayed the CHC’s chair as an “illegal immigrant.” The National Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) deleted the controversial social media post Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Fed chair Powell gives remarks on state of economy
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will give an update on the state of the economy Friday afternoon — less than two months since President Trump returned to the Oval Office. The remarks, part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order on student loan forgiveness program
item.description: As part of the program, those working for the government or a non-profit organization have their loans forgiven.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Linda McMahon: Federal student aid could shift to another department
item.description: Newly confirmed Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon told NewsNation that student loans, Pell Grants and other federal aid "might best be served in another department.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The blame game around Minnesota’s dimmer budget forecast
item.description: Minnesota House Speaker Lisa Demuth, R-Cold Spring, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer ahead of the budget forecast release to talk about how President Trump’s actions are affecting Minnesota and about state GOP plans to reduce fraud and waste.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats react to Trump's speech; state Republicans applaud his vision
item.description: Democrats who represent Nevada in Washington, D.C., condemned what they see as a failure by President Donald Trump to lower prices for Nevada families, while state Republicans applauded his leadership the morning after his address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin audit of Trump win finds not a single voting machine error
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — An audit of the November election won by President Donald Trump in swing-state Wisconsin found that not a single vote was counted incorrectly, altered or missed by tabulating machines.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: The Education Department sent out an email to staffers on Friday saying if they quit by Monday at midnight, they will receive a $25,000 cash payout.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Inside Trump’s federal worker firings
item.description: Some of the removals have targeted a series of officials sure to conduct oversight of Trump’s actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WFLA, NBC
